title: linum spathulatum
intro: Linum spathulatum, also known as coastal linseed or blue flax, is a perennial herb native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its high content of linolenic acid and other fatty acids. The health benefits of Linum spathulatum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its use in treating skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of Linum spathulatum include infusions, tinctures, and oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
